title: quassia polygama
intro: Quassia polygama, also known as quassia amara, is a plant species in the Simaroubaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's roots and bark have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. Quassia polygama has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Quassia polygama can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, or as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
